title: Members using family-specific contribution tiers are skipped
reasoning: The command only selects users with a non-null legacy category, but User::getMonthlyAmount() explicitly supports the newer familyCategory relationship as the primary source of a member's monthly amount. An active member with family_category_id set and category null has a valid monthly contribution amount but will never receive a generated contribution.
reproduction: Create an active user with family_id and family_category_id set, category null, and a FamilyCategory monthly_amount. Run php artisan contributions:generate for that family/month; no Contribution row is created for that user.
recommendation: Select members who have either a family_category_id or a category, and eager-load familyCategory before calling getMonthlyAmount(). Prefer a single scope that matches getMonthlyAmount()'s supported sources of contribution amounts.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature metadata lists no linked tests, so there is no evidence of a console test covering family-specific category members.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Pest console test that creates a family, a FamilyCategory, and an active member assigned only to that FamilyCategory, then runs contributions:generate and asserts a contribution is created with the tier's monthly_amount.
minimumFixScope: Update the member query in app/Console/Commands/GenerateMonthlyContributions.php and add a focused console regression test.
